text: Dinner starts late because a guest is still composing a formal complaint in the library. Everyone else pretends not to hear them practicing it.
Smoke from the kitchen sets off the antique bell system. Weirdly, the guests love this.
Someone posted a clip labeled 'authentic occult retreat.' Mara is on the verge of kicking them out. Jules says it is free marketing.
The ritual today was a little *too* real. Guests are spooked and deeply questioning everything.
There were some mishaps today and while there weren't any serious injuries, certain objects didn't survive. One guest is still being talked down from filing a police report.
Word spreads through the guest wing that today was deeply weird - everyone is thrilled.
The common room is loud in a good way. Guests are making friends and plans with each other.
The evening is polite and brittle. Guests say goodnight like they are leaving a museum where they touched something they should not have.
People are still talking about brunch at supper. Dmitros accepts the praise and beams.
Room service notes mention 'traumatic eggs.' The guests eat crackers in their rooms and call it fasting.
The halls smell like soap with a tinge of ozone. A guest asks who your housekeeper is. You simply spread your hands in front of you and smile.
A guest finds glitter in the bath. Another finds glitter in the bread basket. It is the same glitter.
Someone whispers that this place might actually be real. You take the opportunity to charge extra for candles.
The guests compare notes and decide the occult branding is 'more Etsy than abyss.' Jules looks personally wounded.
No disasters, no miracles. Things settle into that specific quiet where everyone wonders if they are relaxed or afraid.
